members.name: FromDictionary
members.summary: Creates a new GLTFAccessor instance by parsing the given [Dictionary].

members.name: ToDictionary
members.summary: Serializes this GLTFAccessor instance into a [Dictionary].

members.name: AccessorType
members.summary: The glTF accessor type, as an enum.

members.name: BufferView
members.summary: The index of the buffer view this accessor is referencing. If [code]-1[/code], this accessor is not referencing any buffer view.

members.name: ByteOffset
members.summary: The offset relative to the start of the buffer view in bytes.

members.name: ComponentType
members.summary: The glTF component type as an enum. See [enum GLTFComponentType] for possible values. Within the core glTF specification, a value of 5125 or "UNSIGNED_INT" must not be used for any accessor that is not referenced by mesh.primitive.indices.

members.name: Count
members.summary: The number of elements referenced by this accessor.

members.name: Max
members.summary: Maximum value of each component in this accessor.

members.name: Min
members.summary: Minimum value of each component in this accessor.

members.name: Normalized
members.summary: Specifies whether integer data values are normalized before usage.

members.name: SparseCount
members.summary: Number of deviating accessor values stored in the sparse array.

members.name: SparseIndicesBufferView
members.summary: The index of the buffer view with sparse indices. The referenced buffer view MUST NOT have its target or byteStride properties defined. The buffer view and the optional byteOffset MUST be aligned to the componentType byte length.

members.name: SparseIndicesByteOffset
members.summary: The offset relative to the start of the buffer view in bytes.

members.name: SparseIndicesComponentType
members.summary: The indices component data type as an enum. Possible values are 5121 for "UNSIGNED_BYTE", 5123 for "UNSIGNED_SHORT", and 5125 for "UNSIGNED_INT".

members.name: SparseValuesBufferView
members.summary: The index of the bufferView with sparse values. The referenced buffer view MUST NOT have its target or byteStride properties defined.

members.name: SparseValuesByteOffset
members.summary: The offset relative to the start of the bufferView in bytes.

members.name: Type
members.summary: The glTF accessor type, as an [int]. Possible values are [code]0[/code] for "SCALAR", [code]1[/code] for "VEC2", [code]2[/code] for "VEC3", [code]3[/code] for "VEC4", [code]4[/code] for "MAT2", [code]5[/code] for "MAT3", and [code]6[/code] for "MAT4".

values.name: TYPE_MAT2
values.summary: Accessor type "MAT2". For the glTF object model, this maps to "float2x2", represented in the glTF JSON as an array of four floats.

values.name: TYPE_MAT3
values.summary: Accessor type "MAT3". For the glTF object model, this maps to "float3x3", represented in the glTF JSON as an array of nine floats.

values.name: TYPE_MAT4
values.summary: Accessor type "MAT4". For the glTF object model, this maps to "float4x4", represented in the glTF JSON as an array of sixteen floats.

values.name: TYPE_SCALAR
values.summary: Accessor type "SCALAR". For the glTF object model, this can be used to map to a single float, int, or bool value, or a float array.

values.name: TYPE_VEC2
values.summary: Accessor type "VEC2". For the glTF object model, this maps to "float2", represented in the glTF JSON as an array of two floats.

values.name: TYPE_VEC3
values.summary: Accessor type "VEC3". For the glTF object model, this maps to "float3", represented in the glTF JSON as an array of three floats.

values.name: TYPE_VEC4
values.summary: Accessor type "VEC4". For the glTF object model, this maps to "float4", represented in the glTF JSON as an array of four floats.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_DOUBLE_FLOAT
values.summary: Component type "DOUBLE". The value is [code]0x140A[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 8-byte or 64-bit floating-point numbers. This is NOT a core part of the glTF specification, and may not be supported by all glTF importers. May be used by some extensions including [code]KHR_interactivity[/code].

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_HALF_FLOAT
values.summary: Component type "HALF_FLOAT". The value is [code]0x140B[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 2-byte or 16-bit floating-point numbers. This is NOT a core part of the glTF specification, and may not be supported by all glTF importers. May be used by some extensions including [code]KHR_interactivity[/code].

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_NONE
values.summary: Component type "NONE". This is not a valid component type, and is used to indicate that the component type is not set.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_SIGNED_BYTE
values.summary: Component type "BYTE". The value is [code]0x1400[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 1-byte or 8-bit signed integers. This is a core part of the glTF specification.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_SIGNED_INT
values.summary: Component type "INT". The value is [code]0x1404[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 4-byte or 32-bit signed integers. This is NOT a core part of the glTF specification, and may not be supported by all glTF importers. May be used by some extensions including [code]KHR_interactivity[/code].

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_SIGNED_LONG
values.summary: Component type "LONG". The value is [code]0x140E[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 8-byte or 64-bit signed integers. This is NOT a core part of the glTF specification, and may not be supported by all glTF importers. May be used by some extensions including [code]KHR_interactivity[/code].

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_SIGNED_SHORT
values.summary: Component type "SHORT". The value is [code]0x1402[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 2-byte or 16-bit signed integers. This is a core part of the glTF specification.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_SINGLE_FLOAT
values.summary: Component type "FLOAT". The value is [code]0x1406[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 4-byte or 32-bit floating-point numbers. This is a core part of the glTF specification.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_UNSIGNED_BYTE
values.summary: Component type "UNSIGNED_BYTE". The value is [code]0x1401[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 1-byte or 8-bit unsigned integers. This is a core part of the glTF specification.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_UNSIGNED_INT
values.summary: Component type "UNSIGNED_INT". The value is [code]0x1405[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 4-byte or 32-bit unsigned integers. This is a core part of the glTF specification.

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_UNSIGNED_LONG
values.summary: Component type "UNSIGNED_LONG". The value is [code]0x140F[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 8-byte or 64-bit unsigned integers. This is NOT a core part of the glTF specification, and may not be supported by all glTF importers. May be used by some extensions including [code]KHR_interactivity[/code].

values.name: COMPONENT_TYPE_UNSIGNED_SHORT
values.summary: Component type "UNSIGNED_SHORT". The value is [code]0x1403[/code] which comes from OpenGL. This indicates data is stored in 2-byte or 16-bit unsigned integers. This is a core part of the glTF specification.
